content.problemStatement:
Chef has a rectangular field of dimensions N√óMN \times MN√óM which contains grass all over it. (0,0)(0,0)(0,0) is made the bottom left corner of the field. (N,M)(N, M)(N,M) is made the top right corner of the field.

Aliens aim to destroy the field by sending a cow on the field which will eat all the grass.

Chef, in order to protect the field, constructs KKK rectangular electric fences.

An electric fence is represented by x1 y1 x2 y2x_1\space  y_1\space x_2\space y_2x1‚Äč y1‚Äč x2‚Äč y2‚Äč. This denotes an electric fence along the perimeter of the rectangle with bottom left corner as (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč) and top right corner as (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč). The fences can touch or intersect with each other.

The aliens will send the cow to a point PPP on the field. From that point PPP, the cow will graze all the grass it can reach. The cow is intelligent enough to know that electric fence can kill it. Therefore, it will never touch or cross an electric fence. Also, the cow will never leave the field.

The aliens can't see the fences from above and thus will send the cow to any point (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) (not necessarily an integer point) selected uniformly at random on the field. (0‚Č§x‚Č§N,0‚Č§y‚Č§M)(0 \le x \le N, 0 \le y \le M)(0‚Č§x‚Č§N,0‚Č§y‚Č§M)

Chef asks you to calculate the expected area of grass lost due to this alien invasion. Since the Chef doesn't like floating point numbers, he wants you to tell the value of P√óQ‚ąí1 mod 109+7P \times Q^{-1} \space mod \space 10^9+7P√óQ‚ąí1 mod 109+7. Here PQ=\frac{P}{Q} = QP‚Äč= expected grass area destroyed with gcd(P,Q)=1gcd(P,Q)=1gcd(P,Q)=1. Also, Q‚ąí1Q^{-1}Q‚ąí1 is the modular multiplicative inverse of QQQ with respect to 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains three integers N,M,KN, M, KN,M,K denoting the dimensions of the field and the number of rectangular electric fences. Then KKK lines follow.
Each line contains 444 integers x1 y1 x2 y2x_1\space  y_1\space x_2\space y_2x1‚Äč y1‚Äč x2‚Äč y2‚Äč denoting the electric fence.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line the answer to the problem.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: The Chef divides the whole field into 444 equal parts. Therefore 14th\frac{1}{4}^{th}41‚Äčth of the field will be grazed by the cow wherever the cow lands. Expected area lost = 10√ó104=25\frac{10\times 10}{4} = 25410√ó10‚Äč=25.
Testcase 2: The Chef, using 2 fences, divides the whole field into 333 equal parts. Therefore 13th\frac{1}{3}^{th}31‚Äčth of the field will be grazed by the cow wherever the cow lands. Expected area lost = 9√ó93=27\frac{9\times 9}{3} = 2739√ó9‚Äč=27.
